name: French Way - Navarrete Loop
name_original: Vista de Navarrete, Antiguo Hospital de San Juan de Acre, Iglesia de Nuestra Señora de la Asunción – circular
description: Explore Navarrete on this 5.8-mile moderate jogging loop, featuring the Old Hospital of San Juan de Acre and Church of Our Lady of the Assum
region_intro: Jogging around Navarrete offers routes through the heart of La Rioja, characterized by expansive vineyards and verdant crop fields. The region features varied terrain, including paths around a tranquil reservoir and the larger La Grajera Park, which encompasses a lake and shaded areas. Navarrete's location on the Camino de Santiago provides access to well-maintained paths suitable for running, with views of distant hills and agricultural landscapes. Best jogging routes around Navarrete The most popular jogging route is Swamp and La Grajera Park, Ebro Valley – circular, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 46 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir and parkland. Another top favourite among local runners is View of Navarrete, Old Hospital of San Juan de Acre, Church of Our Lady of the Assumption – circular, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.3 km) path that provides panoramic views of Navarrete and its historical landmarks. Local runners also love the Navarrete, Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, Old Hospital of San Juan de Acre – circular, a 2.5 miles (4.0 km) trail leading through the town's historical center, often completed in about 28 minutes. Jogging around Navarrete is defined by vineyards, crop fields, and reservoir paths, with routes offering both flat sections and gentle inclines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy town loops to longer trails around natural parks. The routes in Navarrete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 3800 runners have used komoot to explore Navarrete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Navarrete?
A: Navarrete offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 270 trails documented on komoot. These include 34 easy routes, 186 moderate routes, and 56 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Navarrete?
A: Yes, Navarrete has several easy routes perfect for beginners. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990062088/navarrete-church-of-our-lady-of-the-assumption-old-hospital-of-san-juan-de-acre-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navarrete, Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, Old Hospital of San Juan de Acre – circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.5-mile (4.0 km) trail that takes you through the town's historical center, often completed in about 28 minutes. There are 34 easy routes in total.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Navarrete?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes around Navarrete are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990085336/swamp-and-la-grajera-park-ebro-valley-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Swamp and La Grajera Park, Ebro Valley – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the reservoir and parkland, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990081711/view-of-navarrete-old-hospital-of-san-juan-de-acre-church-of-our-lady-of-the-assumption-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Navarrete, Old Hospital of San Juan de Acre, Church of Our Lady of the Assumption – circular&lt;/a&gt;, providing panoramic views of the town and its historical landmarks.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on jogging trails in Navarrete?
A: Jogging in Navarrete immerses you in the quintessential La Rioja landscape. You'll find routes winding through expansive vineyards and verdant crop fields, offering a scenic and aromatic backdrop. Many trails also feature tranquil reservoir paths, such as those around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and views of distant hills and agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there any running routes near a reservoir or lake?
A: Yes, the area around Navarrete includes a beautiful reservoir, providing excellent opportunities for routes that circle its tranquil waters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990085336/swamp-and-la-grajera-park-ebro-valley-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Swamp and La Grajera Park, Ebro Valley – circular&lt;/a&gt; route is a great example, taking you past the reservoir and through La Grajera Park, which features a large lake. Another option is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38184558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Navarrete&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see while running in Navarrete?
A: Navarrete's routes often combine natural beauty with historical charm. You can run past significant landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990081711/view-of-navarrete-old-hospital-of-san-juan-de-acre-church-of-our-lady-of-the-assumption-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Hospital of San Juan de Acre&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990081711/view-of-navarrete-old-hospital-of-san-juan-de-acre-church-of-our-lady-of-the-assumption-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Our Lady of the Assumption&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5246381" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Cantabria Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4509944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osborne Bull&lt;/a&gt; monument.

Q: Are there any longer running routes, for example, around 10k or more?
A: Yes, Navarrete offers several longer running routes. For a challenging long-distance run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38184558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Navarrete&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 31.5 miles (50.8 km) long. For a route closer to 10k, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990081711/view-of-navarrete-old-hospital-of-san-juan-de-acre-church-of-our-lady-of-the-assumption-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Navarrete, Old Hospital of San Juan de Acre, Church of Our Lady of the Assumption – circular&lt;/a&gt; is 5.7 miles (9.3 km) and offers a moderate challenge.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Navarrete?
A: The running routes in Navarrete are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 3,800 runners have used komoot to explore Navarrete's varied terrain, often praising the picturesque vineyards, well-maintained paths, and the tranquil atmosphere around the reservoir and La Grajera Park.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed jog?
A: Many of Navarrete's routes are suitable for a relaxed jog or a family outing. The town's location on the Camino de Santiago means access to well-maintained, relatively flat paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990062088/navarrete-church-of-our-lady-of-the-assumption-old-hospital-of-san-juan-de-acre-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navarrete, Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, Old Hospital of San Juan de Acre – circular&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.5-mile loop through the town center, perfect for a leisurely pace.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Navarrete?
A: Navarrete, located in La Rioja, generally offers pleasant conditions for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery as the vineyards change color. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended. Winter runs are also possible, though some paths might be damp.

Q: Are there any shaded routes for running during warmer weather?
A: Yes, La Grajera Park, located a short distance from Navarrete, offers expansive shaded areas, making it ideal for runs during warmer weather. Routes that pass through or around the park, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990085336/swamp-and-la-grajera-park-ebro-valley-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Swamp and La Grajera Park, Ebro Valley – circular&lt;/a&gt;, provide a cooler environment.

Q: Where can I find more information about the Navarrete region?
A: For more general information about Navarrete and the surrounding La Rioja region, including cultural insights and other activities, you can visit the official tourism website for Spain: &lt;a href="https://www.spain.info/en/destination/navarrete/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;spain.info/en/destination/navarrete/&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: The vineyards even extend along the slopes near Logroño.

Navarrete is a town on the Way of St. James in the autonomous region of La Rioja. Due to its monuments and historical significance, the town centre has been declared a Conjunto histórico-artístico. After walking towards the town for what seems like an eternity, you are rewarded with a magical town that has one of the most beautiful cathedrals on the Way of St. James.

Along the Camino de Santiago several 'hospitals' were built in the Middle Ages, which were essentially places of rest and food, although they also provided security and care for pilgrims. This was founded in 1185 in this part of the Camino Frances.

This church is dedicated to the Blessed Mother of God Santa Maria. The addition "de la Asunción" of the patronage refers to the Feast of the Annunciation to Mary (feast day is March 25th, exactly 9 months before Christmas), when the angel of the Lord brought her the message that she was the Son of God - Jesus Christ - to bring into the world.
According to the account in the first chapter of the Gospel of Luke, this happened in Nazareth (Israel). There is a large church on the spot where this happened - according to a tradition of around two thousand years - which can therefore be used as the 'origin patronal church' for the title of this church here.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This circular jogging route is approximately 9.25 kilometers long and generally takes about 1 hour and 4 minutes to complete at a jogging pace.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or those new to jogging?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as moderate for jogging but features easily-accessible paths with gentle inclines, making it suitable for individuals of various fitness levels, including beginners. No special fitness level or sure-footedness is required.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally flat or has gentle inclines, described as 'easily-accessible paths'. You can expect a comfortable surface suitable for jogging.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. You'll enjoy a View of the Ebro Valley Vineyards and a View of Navarrete. Key historical landmarks include the Ruins of the Hospital of San Juan de Acre and the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other paths, including the famous Camino Francés - 03 Logroño a Burgos, Camino de Santiago, and the Ignatian Way.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the sources don't explicitly state leash rules, trails in this region are generally dog-friendly. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to respect other users and the environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: The La Rioja region experiences pleasant weather in spring and autumn, making these seasons ideal for jogging. Summers can be hot, and winters might be chilly, so check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to jog this circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Navarrete?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route through Navarrete, you can typically find parking within the town itself. Look for public parking areas near the historic center or close to the main landmarks mentioned in the trail name, such as the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jog on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate length and ease of the trail, comfortable jogging shoes, appropriate athletic wear, and a water bottle are recommended. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also advisable, especially during sunnier months.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the jogging route?
faqs.answer: Navarrete is a town with amenities, and you will find cafes and restaurants within the town center, which the circular route passes through or near. These can be good spots for refreshments before or after your jog.
